本人郑重声明：本论文的所有工作，是在导师的指导下，由作者本人独立完成的。有关观点、方法、数据和文献的引用已在文中指出，并与参考文献相对应。除文中已注明引用的内容外，本论文不包含任何其他个人或集体已经公开发表的作品成果。对本文的研究做出重要贡献的个人和集体，均已在文中以明确方式标明。本人完全意识
到本声明的法律结果由本人承担。

本人完全了解学校保护知识产权的有关规定，即在校攻读学位期间论文工作的知识产权属于哈尔滨工程大学。哈尔滨工程大学有权保留并向国家有关部门或机构送交论文的复印件。本人允许哈尔滨工程大学将论文的部分或全部内容编入有关数据库进行检索，可采用影印、缩印或扫描等复制手段保存和汇编本学位论文，可以公布论文的全部内容。同时本人保证毕业后结合学位论文研究课题再撰写的论文一律注明作者第一署名单位为哈尔滨工程大学。涉密学位论文待解密后适用本声明。

national departments or institutions. I allow Harbin Engineering University to include part or all of the content of the thesis in relevant databases for retrieval, and to use photocopying,

microfilming, scanning, or other means of reproduction to preserve and compile this thesis. The full content of the thesis can be made available to the public. This statement is applicable to confidential theses after they are declassified.
This thesis can be submitted to relevant departments for preservation and compilation by Harbin
The study of wave propagation characteristics near the ocean bottom is a prominent field in oceanography.
Water wave; Ground wave; ship collision
海洋声传播研究长期以来一直是海洋声学学科中的热门领域，尤其是在海底附近的水波和地波传播特性方面，如时域波形和传播损失等。尽管目前在此领域取得了一定进展，但对于水波和地波的分离研究甚少。这些问题的解决不仅对海洋声场分析有重要意义，还对海洋资源开发和海底地震灾害预测具有实用价值。
在这一背景下，本文探讨了
水波；底波；模态频散；谱元法；水波分离
2.1 Review of water wave and ground wave
3.2 Ground/water waves and Airy waves theory
3.3 Spectral Element Method Model based on SPECFEM2D
Numerical study of Ground/ Water waves
4.2 Separation of ground and water waves

life, nurturing countless species and abundant energy resources.

查找路径：钉钉全院文件夹——22级毕设——IP 辅导PPT资料——2022级IP 辅导资料
In shallow water environments, the propagation of sound waves is determined by the unique physical
properties of the environment, exhibiting distinct propagation characteristics (Pekeris 17).

The project focuses on the acoustic propagation characteristics of broadband sound sources in shallow water, aiming to study theoretically and experimentally the method of separating the ground wave from water wave and analyse the transmission efficiency.
To comprehensively understand and simulate the interaction between ground and water waves, the research has established the following specific objectives:
This thesis analyses water and ground waves near the ocean bottom from numerical simulation and experimental data processing perspectives.
2.1 Review of water wave and ground

As early as 1948, Pekeris proposed a method for solving the normal mode acoustic field in the context of exploring shallow water waveguide problems composed of a uniform fluid layer and a semi-infinite liquid ocean bottom layer (Pekeris 43).
the flexible handling capability of the finite element method with the high-speed convergence characteristics of the pseudo-spectral method, providing an efficient solution for the
numerical simulation of sound wave propagation
In studying sound propagation in shallow waters, broadband point sources are taken into consideration.
The normal mode theory was first developed by Pekeris as a simple two-layer fluid ocean model (Jensen et al. 102–33).
The Pekeris waveguide, proposed by Pekeris, is a waveguide environment consisting of a uniform
Fig. 3.3 First-Order Mode Group Velocity-Frequency Relationship
Fig. 3.4 Theoretical contribution of the first mode to the disturbance, from Chaim Leib Pekeris, "Theory of Propagation of Explosive Sound in Shallow Water" (1948 94).
3.3 Spectral Element Method Model based on

The core idea of the Spectral Element Method is to first divide the entire computational area into a finite number of elements and then apply the Pseudospectral Method, distributing non-uniform nodes within each element.
When conducting ocean acoustic field modelling, it is necessary to determine parameters such
Fig. 3.6 Shallow water wave guide model
This chapter primarily conducts theoretical analyses on the following three aspects:
In order to measure the transmission efficiency of the water wave-ground wave channel, the
layer model created in Section 3.3 using the spectral element method.
The separation of water waves from ground waves first necessitates the modal resolution of
received by the horizontal array stations.
The method of t - p transformation involves stacking signals in the xt- domain according to different intercepts t and slopes p (Zhou and Greenhalgh 1133–49).
This chapter aims to improve the comprehension of how sound waves propagate in the horizontally
As shown in Chapter 4, it presents a simulation of a three-layer horizontally stratified ocean bottom using the two-dimensional Spectral Element Method.
The College of Underwater Acoustic Engineering at Harbin Engineering University conducted a comprehensive marine experiment in a specific sea area.
Similar to numerical studies, the processing of experimental data primarily relies on the water-ground
This chapter focuses on the processing and analysis of experimental data, with the aim of separating the ground waves and water waves.
The aim of the study is to separate the ground waves and water waves in a horizontally stratified environment.
